subtasks.description: Evaluate developer's production database access request against security and compliance policies. Check: (1) Is direct production access permitted for debugging? (2) Does developer have required authorization/role? (3) Are there mandatory approval workflows or audit requirements? (4) What are conditional guardrails (e.g., time limits, read-only access, IP restrictions)?

plan.cost_rationale: Single subtask because the query presents one discrete governance decision: whether to allow/deny/conditionally permit developer access to production database. This is a policy evaluation task requiring cross-policy reasoning (security, compliance, and operational policies likely interact), so mid-tier is appropriate. No additional source reconciliation or multi-tool orchestration is needed—this is purely a policy gate decision.

output.reason: Developer 'dev-alice' lacks Manager approval and on-call role required by POL-401 for production DB access. POL-402 mandates debugging be performed in the designated staging environment, not production. Recommend redirecting debug activity to staging; if production access is truly required, obtain Manager approval and assume on-call role.
applied_constraints: Redirect debugging to staging environment per POL-402
Require Manager approval and on-call role assignment per POL-401 before any production access
Log denied access attempt for audit
